summary: Matched and improved multiple video intro/cutscene functions across src/melee/vi, with supporting header/signature updates in gr, pl, mn, and item headers. The bot report records 15 new matches and 23 improvements, while later author commentary notes broken/regressed matches would be fixed, so the slice supports progress but not a clean final validation story for every touched function.

what_changed: Primary PR body states the goal was to match various video intro/cutscene functions across vi module files.
Bot report records matched-code progress of +0.09% and +3384 bytes, with 15 new matches and 23 improvements.
Major vi files changed: vi1201v1.c, vi1202.c, vi0801.c, vi0601.c, vi1201v2.c, vi0501.c, vi0502.c, vi0102.c, vi0401.c, vi0402.c, vi1101.c and associated headers/static headers.
Header/signature surfaces were updated in vi headers, grbigblueroute.h, grcorneria.h, mncharsel.h, plbonuslib.c/h, and item headers.
Several source-local extern declarations for it_804D6D40 were removed from item C files and declarations were added to item headers; it_2725.c changed the symbol type to f32*.

Some generated-looking access was cleaned up where evidence was local: vi0402 replaced M2C_FIELD writes into un_804D6F84 with array indexing, and vi0401 used a typed local pointer plus HSD_ASSERT before assigning a Vec3.
Loaded diff excerpt includes formatting-only or style cleanup in item files, such as cast spacing and line wrapping in itdosei.c and item headers.

smart_moves.text: The PR updated cross-module prototypes alongside vi callsite work instead of hiding call signatures behind local declarations.
evidence_refs: PR body: necessary cross-module header updates for gr/ and pl/ signatures used by vi functions

smart_moves.text: The PR used existing scene/camera/fog/light/model helper APIs in recovered vi OnEnter functions rather than leaving every setup step as placeholder code.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: vi0501.c un_8031DE58_OnEnter uses lbArchive_LoadSymbols, HSD_FogLoadDesc, GObj_Create, HSD_GObjObject_80390A70, GObj_SetupGXLink, HSD_CObjAddAnim, HSD_JObjLoadJoint

smart_moves.text: Where byte-level storage was visibly an array, the diff replaced M2C_FIELD writes with indexed array writes.
The bot report gives narrow symbol-level progress, making it clear which functions reached 100% and which remained partial.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev[bot] report with 15 new matches and 23 improvements

decomp_lessons.lesson: When vi sequence functions call into stage/player/menu/item code, recover and update the owning headers for proven signatures rather than relying on UNK_RET, UNK_PARAMS, or source-local externs.
evidence_refs: PR body: cross-module header updates for gr/ and pl/ signatures used by vi functions

decomp_lessons.lesson: Prefer typed or indexed data access when the storage shape is clear; the PR shows both direct array indexing for byte slots and typed locals for scene data.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: vi0402.c un_8031D9E4 replaced M2C_FIELD writes with un_804D6F84 array indexing
diff_excerpt: vi0401.c uses un_804D6F58_t* ptr and pos = ptr->x38

decomp_lessons.lesson: Record partial-match status honestly: this PR had many complete matches, but some large recovered functions remained partial and one author comment explicitly mentioned broken/regressed matches.
evidence_refs: bot_report: un_8031FD18_OnEnter 82.73%, un_8032233C 58.24%, other partial improvements
issue_comment: oops, will fix the broken/regressed matches

naming_conventions.convention: Recovered vi functions kept address-style names such as un_8031FD18_OnEnter, un_8032233C, fn_8031FAA8, and file-prefixed vi0102_CameraCallback where semantics were not fully established.
evidence_refs: review_comments: hunks for un_8031FD18_OnEnter and un_8032233C
diff_excerpt: vi0102_CameraCallback and vi0501 function names

naming_conventions.convention: Simple local role names were used for clear HSD object roles, including gobj, jobj, cobj, fog_gobj, light_gobj, camera_gobj, and model_gobj.
Header declarations retained address comments for globals and functions, such as /* 4D6D40 */ extern f32* it_804D6D40 and /* 03FDA0 */ void pl_8003FDA0(int, int).

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use local variables to influence lifetime/scope for matching, as seen with vi0402 hoisting light_gobj and vi0401 introducing ptr before an HSD_ASSERT and Vec3 copy.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: This is supported as a tactic visible in the diff, but the slice does not include objdiff details explaining why each lifetime change was necessary.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: For vi setup routines, construct the source around standard asset and HSD object-loading calls: archive load, fog/light/camera/model GObj creation, animation request, and process setup.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: The bot report shows near-complete progress for this function, but not a full 100% match.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Replace raw M2C_FIELD byte writes with indexed byte-array writes when the global behaves like an array.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: Consistent with typed-fields-over-pointer-math; no broader claim about all globals is supported.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK appeared in touched item code but the excerpt does not establish it was newly introduced for the vi match.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: Do not generalize this as accepted tactic evidence for the PR.

review_feedback.interpretation: Feedback targeted raw input/offset-style handling in un_8031FD18_OnEnter; struct extraction was preferred, with M2C_FIELD as fallback.

review_feedback.interpretation: Feedback targeted data/offset access in un_8032233C and nearby un_803224DC changes; typed struct recovery was preferred.

notes: The raw diff loaded in the prompt is truncated, so several changed files, especially vi0502.c, vi0601.c, vi0801.c, vi1101.c, vi1201v2.c, and full vi1201v1/vi1202 bodies, are represented mostly by changed-file metadata and bot report entries rather than full code excerpts.

The decomp-dev bot report supports match progress at the time of that comment, but the later author comment says broken/regressed matches would be fixed; the slice does not show which regressions existed or their final resolution.
Review comments are authored by the PR author in the loaded data; no separate reviewer intent should be inferred.
No build logs, objdiff screenshots, or final checkdiff output beyond the bot report are included.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Cross-module vi matching should update owning headers when signatures are proven
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2073 matched vi intro/cutscene functions and updated gr, pl, mn, and item header declarations instead of relying only on local declarations or UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
evidence_refs: PR body: cross-module header updates for gr/ and pl/ signatures used by vi functions
diff_excerpt: grbigblueroute.h, grcorneria.h, plbonuslib.c/h, mncharsel.h, item headers

accepted_candidate_records.title: Prefer typed struct/array access over raw offset access in vi matching
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2073 contains direct cleanup from M2C_FIELD byte writes to array indexing, and inline feedback on two large vi functions preferred pulling data out into a struct, with M2C_FIELD only as fallback.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep match-progress claims symbol-specific when a PR mixes complete and partial vi function recovery
accepted_candidate_records.text: The bot report lists exact symbols that reached 100% and others that only improved partially; later author commentary mentions broken/regressed matches, so broad claims of full validation are not supported by this slice.
evidence_refs: issue_comment: oops, will fix the broken/regressed matches

source_update_candidates.title: Struct extraction preferred over M2C_FIELD for repeated raw data access
source_update_candidates.text: Two PR 2073 inline comments say to pull raw access into a struct, preferred, or use M2C_FIELD. This reinforces the existing typed-fields-over-pointer-math standard and may be useful as a concrete historical example rather than a new rule.
source_update_candidates.reason: Curator may add this PR as supporting evidence/example for the existing typed-field standard.

source_update_candidates.title: Do not overstate bot-reported progress when later PR comments mention regressions
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2073 includes a bot report with many symbol-level matches and a later author comment about fixing broken/regressed matches. This may be a useful example for verification ledger guidance: preserve exact timing and scope of verification evidence.
evidence_refs: issue_comment: oops, will fix the broken/regressed matches
source_update_candidates.reason: Candidate example for existing verification-and-regression-ledger standard; not enough to create a separate accepted rule.

source_update_candidates.title: src/melee/vi sequence files depend on gr/pl/mn/item signatures
source_update_candidates.text: PR body says vi function matching required cross-module header updates for gr and pl signatures, and the diff also shows mn and item header/signature changes.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path-fact candidate about vi matching touching cross-module declarations; needs curator verification against current source before promotion.

rejection_notes: Do not claim every function in the PR matched; the bot report shows several partial improvements and the later author comment mentions broken/regressed matches.
Do not infer reviewer intent beyond the two loaded review comments; both are authored by itsgrimetime in the slice.
Do not promote the it_804D6D40 f32* type as globally validated solely from this intake; the diff shows the change but not the underlying symbol/type proof.
Do not create a new standard favoring M2C_FIELD; the evidence says struct extraction was preferred and M2C_FIELD was fallback.
